preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 66% to win, with Uzbekistan positioned as a substantial underdog at 9%. This 57-percentage-point gap reflects a clear hierarchy, though the model assigns Uzbekistan a non-trivial chance to either draw or upset on a neutral ground.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Portugal as the commanding favorite, yet a draw at 25% remains a meaningful outcome — more probable than an Uzbekistan victory. The match is unlikely to be heavily one-sided in expectation; competitive margins exist across the result distribution, with the draw probability anchoring a scenario in which neither side dominates decisively.

The market consensus across sportsbooks runs substantially more confident in Portugal, placing them at 76% compared to the model's 66%. The 10-percentage-point gap widens further in crowd-sourced pricing (Polymarket: 79%), with both market sources trimming draw odds to 13–15%. The dashboard offers no resolution between these views; the divergence suggests market participants believe Portugal's advantage is steeper than the model's estimate.
